steps.title: Paraphrase the inefficiency
steps.content: The student must paraphrase Mercer and Whitman's explanation of why barter is inefficient. Their quotation says a trader must find someone who both has what the trader wants and wants what the trader has. The key idea is the difficult matching of both parties' desires.

steps.title: Avoid definition-only choices
steps.content: Choice A and Choice C mainly restate that barter involves trading goods without money, but they do not explain why this creates inefficiency. Choice D shifts to the later idea that barter economies can become monetary economies.

steps.title: Select the matching paraphrase
steps.content: The remaining choice captures the need for a precise alignment of desires, which paraphrases the quoted explanation of why barter wastes time. The answer is B.
